```{python}
            patience=RL_PAT,
            min_lr=KERAS_TUNE["min_lr"]
        ),
        keras.callbacks.ModelCheckpoint(
            filepath=best_weights_path,
            monitor="val_loss",
            save_best_only=True,
            save_weights_only=True
        ),
    ]

# write run info
def write_run_info(run_dir, extra: dict):
    info = {
        "train_mode": TRAIN_MODE,
        "timestamp": datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S"),
        "num_cols": list(num_cols_nn),
        "cat_cols": list(cat_cols_nn),
        "train_medians": {k: float(v) for k, v in train_medians.to_dict().items()},
        **extra
    }
    atomic_write_json(run_dir / "run_info.json", info)

# Minimal dataset wrapper (prevents intermittent tuner/batchnorm shape issues on full runs)
AUTOTUNE = tf.data.AUTOTUNE

def make_ds(inputs, y, batch_size: int, training: bool):
    ds = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices((inputs, y))
    if training:
        ds = ds.shuffle(min(len(y), 50_000), seed=SEED, reshuffle_each_iteration=True)
        ds = ds.batch(batch_size, drop_remainder=True)   
    else:
        ds = ds.batch(batch_size, drop_remainder=False)
    return ds.prefetch(AUTOTUNE)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 1) SKIP mode: load latest if available
# -------------------------------------------------------
if TRAIN_MODE == "skip":
    if latest_path.exists():
        print("✅ TRAIN_MODE='skip' — loading latest Keras model:\n ", latest_path)
        model = keras.models.load_model(latest_path, compile=False)
    else:
        print("⏭️ TRAIN_MODE='skip' — no latest Keras model found, skipping.")
        model = None

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 2) FULL mode: load latest unless retraining
# -------------------------------------------------------
elif (TRAIN_MODE == "full") and latest_path.exists() and (not RETRAIN_KERAS):
    print("✅ Loading existing Keras latest model (RETRAIN_KERAS=False, TRAIN_MODE='full'):\n ", latest_path)
    model = keras.models.load_model(latest_path, compile=False)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Otherwise TRAIN/TUNE
# -------------------------------------------------------
else:
    if not WILL_TRAIN_KERAS:
        raise RuntimeError("WILL_TRAIN_KERAS=False but training branch entered. Check logic.")

    run_ts = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    KERAS_RUN_DIR = KERAS_RUNS_DIR / f"run_{run_ts}_{TRAIN_MODE}"
    KERAS_RUN_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    versioned_path = KERAS_RUN_DIR / "keras_model.keras"

    # ----------------------------
    # Tuner directories (SEPARATE)
    # ----------------------------
    hb_dir = KERAS_RUN_DIR / "tuning" / "hyperband"
    bo_dir = KERAS_RUN_DIR / "tuning" / "bayes"
    hb_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    bo_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # ----------------------------
    # Budgets per mode
    # ----------------------------
    if SMOKE:
        EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["epochs_smoke"]
        HB_MAX_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["hb_max_epochs_smoke"]
        BAYES_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_epochs_smoke"]
        BAYES_TRIALS = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_max_trials_smoke"]
        BATCH = KERAS_TUNE["batch_smoke"]
        HB_EXEC = KERAS_TUNE["hb_executions_per_trial"]
        BAYES_EXEC = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_executions_per_trial"]
    else:
        EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["epochs_full"]
        HB_MAX_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["hb_max_epochs_full"]
        BAYES_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_epochs_full"]
        BAYES_TRIALS = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_max_trials_full"]
        BATCH = KERAS_TUNE["batch_full"]
        HB_EXEC = KERAS_TUNE["hb_executions_per_trial"]
        BAYES_EXEC = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_executions_per_trial"]

    # ----------------------------
    # Optional SMOKE subsampling (keeps tuner, but faster)
    # ----------------------------
    if SMOKE and KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_subsample", False):
        rs = np.random.RandomState(SEED)

        ntr = min(int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_train_n", 20000)), len(y_train_nn))
        nva = min(int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_val_n", 5000)), len(y_val_nn))

        idx_tr = rs.choice(len(y_train_nn), size=ntr, replace=False)
        idx_va = rs.choice(len(y_val_nn),   size=nva, replace=False)

        train_inputs_run = {k: v[idx_tr] for k, v in train_inputs.items()}
        y_train_run = y_train_nn[idx_tr]

        val_inputs_run = {k: v[idx_va] for k, v in val_inputs.items()}
        y_val_run = y_val_nn[idx_va]

        print(
            f"🧪 Smoke subsample enabled: train={ntr:,} val={nva:,} | "
            f"batch={BATCH} | hb_epochs={HB_MAX_EPOCHS} | bayes_epochs={BAYES_EPOCHS} | bayes_trials={BAYES_TRIALS}"
        )
    else:
        train_inputs_run, y_train_run = train_inputs, y_train_nn
        val_inputs_run, y_val_run = val_inputs, y_val_nn

    # Create datasets once and reuse for tuner + final fit
    train_ds = make_ds(train_inputs_run, y_train_run, BATCH, training=True)
    val_ds   = make_ds(val_inputs_run,   y_val_run,   BATCH, training=False)

    # Objective: minimize validation loss in log space
    objective = kt.Objective("val_loss", direction="min")

    print("num feature count =", len(num_cols_nn))
    print("cat feature count =", len(cat_cols_nn))
    print("train numeric shape =", train_inputs["numeric"].shape)
    print("one cat example shape =", train_inputs[cat_cols_nn[0]].shape if cat_cols_nn else None)
    print("y_train shape =", y_train_nn.shape)

    # ----------------------------
    # TUNING STAGE 1 (coarse): Hyperband
    # ----------------------------
    print("🧭 TUNING STAGE 1 (coarse): Hyperband")
    gc.collect()

    hb_best_weights_path = str(hb_dir / "best_tuning.weights.h5")
    hb_callbacks = make_callbacks(hb_best_weights_path, TRAIN_MODE)

    hb = kt.Hyperband(
        hypermodel=build_model_from_hp,
        objective=objective,
        max_epochs=HB_MAX_EPOCHS,
        factor=KERAS_TUNE["hb_factor"],
        executions_per_trial=HB_EXEC,
        directory=str(hb_dir),
        project_name=f"run_{run_ts}",
        overwrite=True,
    )

    hb.search(
        train_ds,
        validation_data=val_ds,
        epochs=HB_MAX_EPOCHS,
        callbacks=hb_callbacks,
        verbose=0,
    )

    best_hp_hb = hb.get_best_hyperparameters(1)[0]
    print("✅ Best HP from Hyperband:", best_hp_hb.values)

    # ----------------------------
    # TUNING STAGE 2 (fine): BayesianOptimization
    # ----------------------------
    print("🎯 TUNING STAGE 2 (fine): BayesianOptimization")
    tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
    gc.collect()

    bo_best_weights_path = str(bo_dir / "best_tuning.weights.h5")
    bo_callbacks = make_callbacks(bo_best_weights_path, TRAIN_MODE)

    bo = kt.BayesianOptimization(
        hypermodel=build_model_from_hp,
        objective=objective,
        max_trials=BAYES_TRIALS,
        executions_per_trial=BAYES_EXEC,
        directory=str(bo_dir),
        project_name=f"run_{run_ts}",
        overwrite=True,
    )

    bo.search(
        train_ds,
        validation_data=val_ds,
        epochs=BAYES_EPOCHS,   
        callbacks=bo_callbacks,
        verbose=0,
    )

    best_hp = bo.get_best_hyperparameters(1)[0]
    print("🏆 Best HP from BayesianOptimization:", best_hp.values)

    # ----------------------------
    # FINAL FIT using best HP (FROM SCRATCH)
    # ----------------------------
    print("🔁 Final training with best hyperparameters (from scratch)")
    tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
    gc.collect()

    model = build_model_from_hp(best_hp)

    final_best_weights_path = str(KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_final.weights.h5")
    final_callbacks = make_callbacks(final_best_weights_path, TRAIN_MODE)

    history = model.fit(
        train_ds,
        validation_data=val_ds,
        epochs=EPOCHS,
        verbose=0,
        callbacks=final_callbacks
    )

    # Load best FINAL weights explicitly (weights-only checkpoint)
    if os.path.exists(final_best_weights_path):
        model.load_weights(final_best_weights_path)
        print("✅ Loaded best FINAL weights:", final_best_weights_path)

    # Save FULL model artifact
    model.save(versioned_path)
    print("💾 Saved versioned FULL model:", versioned_path)

    # Update "latest" only for full mode
    if TRAIN_MODE == "full":
        model.save(latest_path)
        print("✅ Updated latest FULL model:", latest_path)
    else:
        print("🚫 Smoke run: NOT updating latest pointer.")

    # Persist tuning results + chosen hp
    write_run_info(KERAS_RUN_DIR, extra={
        "tuning": {
            "strategy": "hyperband_then_bayes",
            "hyperband_best": dict(best_hp_hb.values),
            "bayes_best": dict(best_hp.values),
            "hb_max_epochs": int(HB_MAX_EPOCHS),
            "bayes_epochs": int(BAYES_EPOCHS),
            "bayes_max_trials": int(BAYES_TRIALS),
            "hyperband_dir": str(hb_dir),
            "bayes_dir": str(bo_dir),
            "smoke_subsample": bool(SMOKE and KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_subsample", False)),
            "smoke_train_n": int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_train_n", 0)) if SMOKE else None,
            "smoke_val_n": int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_val_n", 0)) if SMOKE else None,
            "batch": int(BATCH),
            "final_epochs_cap": int(EPOCHS),
        }
    })

    atomic_write_json(KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_hp.json", dict(best_hp.values))
    print("🧾 Wrote best_hp.json:", KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_hp.json")
```

#### Keras Evaluation 
```{python}
# ==========================================================
# Keras Model Evaluation
# ==========================================================
# ----------------------------
# Step 7: Evaluate (dollars) with full + clipped
# + Permutation importance (per-feature + optional subsample)
# + Training curves (if history exists)
# Ensures globals for metrics writer cell are set.
# ----------------------------

if ("model" not in globals()) or (model is None):
    print("⚠️ Keras model is not available — skipping evaluation.")
else:
    # ----------------------------
    # Helper: safe predict -> dollars
    # ----------------------------
    def _predict_dollars(inputs: dict) -> np.ndarray:
        pred_log = model.predict(
            inputs,
            batch_size=KERAS_TUNE["pred_batch"],
            verbose=0
        ).ravel()

        # Safety: replace NaN/Inf if something went wrong
        pred_log = np.nan_to_num(pred_log, nan=0.0, posinf=15.0, neginf=6.0)

        # Convert to dollars in float64
        return np.expm1(pred_log.astype("float64"))

    # ----------------------------
    # TEST metrics (full + clipped)
    # ----------------------------
    true_test = np.expm1(y_test_nn.astype("float64"))
    pred_test = _predict_dollars(test_inputs)

    keras_test_m = metrics_full_and_clipped(true_test, pred_test, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99)
    display(metrics_to_df(keras_test_m, "Keras (Test)"))

    # ----------------------------
    # Train+Val metrics (full + clipped)
    # ----------------------------
    X_trainval_nn = pd.concat([X_train_nn, X_val_nn], axis=0)
    y_trainval_nn = np.concatenate([y_train_nn, y_val_nn], axis=0)
    trainval_inputs = to_input_dict(X_trainval_nn)

    true_trainval = np.expm1(y_trainval_nn.astype("float64"))
    pred_trainval = _predict_dollars(trainval_inputs)

    keras_trainval_m = metrics_full_and_clipped(true_trainval, pred_trainval, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99)
    display(metrics_to_df(keras_trainval_m, "Keras (Train+Val)"))

    # Globals for your metrics
    keras_test_full = keras_test_m["full"]
    keras_test_clipped = keras_test_m["clipped"]
    keras_trainval_full = keras_trainval_m["full"]
    keras_trainval_clipped = keras_trainval_m["clipped"]

    # ----------------------------
    # Permutation importance (per-feature + optional subsample)
    # - Numeric features: permute 1 numeric column at a time.
    # - Categorical features: permute 1 categorical input at a time (each cat col is its own key).
    # - Optional row subsample for speed.
    # ----------------------------
    def permutation_importance_keras_per_feature(
        base_inputs: dict,
        y_true_dollars: np.ndarray,
        *,
        n_repeats: int = 3,
        seed: int = 42,
        max_rows: int | None = 20000,   # speed knob: set None for full test set
        include_categoricals: bool = True,
    ):
        rs = np.random.RandomState(seed)

        # ----------------------------
        # 0) Subsample rows for speed (optional)
        # ----------------------------
        N = len(y_true_dollars)
        if (max_rows is not None) and (N > max_rows):
            idx_rows = rs.choice(N, size=max_rows, replace=False)

            sub_inputs = {}
            for k, v in base_inputs.items():
                if k == "numeric":
                    sub_inputs[k] = v[idx_rows, :]
                else:
                    sub_inputs[k] = v[idx_rows]
            y_sub = y_true_dollars[idx_rows]
        else:
            sub_inputs = {k: v.copy() for k, v in base_inputs.items()}
            y_sub = y_true_dollars

        # ----------------------------
        # 1) Baseline RMSE (on the chosen subset)
        # ----------------------------
        base_pred = _predict_dollars(sub_inputs)
        base_rmse = float(np.sqrt(np.mean((y_sub - base_pred) ** 2)))

        rows = []

        # ----------------------------
        # 2) Numeric per-column importance
        # ----------------------------
        Xnum = sub_inputs["numeric"]
        d = int(Xnum.shape[1])

        # Nice labels if num_cols_nn matches the numeric matrix width
        if ("num_cols_nn" in globals()) and (len(num_cols_nn) == d):
            num_names = list(num_cols_nn)
        else:
            num_names = [f"num_{j}" for j in range(d)]

        for j in range(d):
            deltas = []
            for _ in range(n_repeats):
                pert = {k: v.copy() for k, v in sub_inputs.items()}

                idx = rs.permutation(len(y_sub))

                pert_num = pert["numeric"]
                pert_num[:, j] = pert_num[idx, j]
                pert["numeric"] = pert_num

                pred = _predict_dollars(pert)
                rmse = float(np.sqrt(np.mean((y_sub - pred) ** 2)))
                deltas.append(rmse - base_rmse)

            rows.append({
                "feature": num_names[j],
                "feature_type": "numeric",
                "rmse_increase": float(np.mean(deltas)),
                "rmse_increase_std": float(np.std(deltas)),
            })

        # ----------------------------
        # 3) Categorical per-key importance (optional)
        # ----------------------------
        if include_categoricals:
            for key in sub_inputs.keys():
                if key == "numeric":
                    continue

                deltas = []
                for _ in range(n_repeats):
                    pert = {k: v.copy() for k, v in sub_inputs.items()}
                    idx = rs.permutation(len(y_sub))

                    pert[key] = pert[key][idx]

                    pred = _predict_dollars(pert)
                    rmse = float(np.sqrt(np.mean((y_sub - pred) ** 2)))
                    deltas.append(rmse - base_rmse)

                rows.append({
                    "feature": key,
                    "feature_type": "categorical",
                    "rmse_increase": float(np.mean(deltas)),
                    "rmse_increase_std": float(np.std(deltas)),
                })

        df_pi = (
            pd.DataFrame(rows)
            .sort_values("rmse_increase", ascending=False)
            .reset_index(drop=True)
        )
        return base_rmse, df_pi

    try:
        base_rmse_pi, pi_df = permutation_importance_keras_per_feature(
            test_inputs,
            true_test,
            n_repeats=3,
            seed=SEED if "SEED" in globals() else 42,
            max_rows=20000,               
            include_categoricals=True    
        )
        print(f"Baseline TEST RMSE ($) [subset for PI]: {base_rmse_pi:,.0f}")
        display(pi_df.head(30))
    except Exception as e:
        print("⚠️ Permutation importance failed:", e)

    # ----------------------------
    # Training curves (if this run trained in-session)
    # ----------------------------
    if ("history" in globals()) and (history is not None):
        hist = history.history if hasattr(history, "history") else {}
        if ("loss" in hist) or ("val_loss" in hist):
            plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))
            if "loss" in hist:
                plt.plot(hist["loss"], label="loss")
            if "val_loss" in hist:
                plt.plot(hist["val_loss"], label="val_loss")
            plt.xlabel("Epoch")
            plt.ylabel("Loss")
            plt.title("Keras Training Curves")
            plt.legend()
            plt.tight_layout()
            plt.show()
        else:
            print("ℹ️ history exists but does not contain loss/val_loss keys.")
    else:
        print("ℹ️ No training history available (likely loaded model or training skipped).")

    print("✅ Done: Keras NN tuned/trained and evaluated (with permutation importance + curves).")
```

### Metrics Savepoint
```{python}
# -------------------------
# Save Metrics
# Standard schema across models:
#   metrics[model]["trainval"]["full"/"clipped"]
#   metrics[model]["test"]["full"/"clipped"]
# -------------------------

metrics = {}

# ---- Meta ----
metrics["_meta"] = {
    "timestamp": datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),
    "train_mode": TRAIN_MODE if "TRAIN_MODE" in globals() else None,
    "updates_latest": (TRAIN_MODE == "full") if "TRAIN_MODE" in globals() else None,
    "rows": {
        "train": int(len(X_train)) if "X_train" in globals() else None,
        "val": int(len(X_val)) if "X_val" in globals() else None,
        "test": int(len(X_test)) if "X_test" in globals() else None,
        "total": int(len(X)) if "X" in globals() else None,
    },
    "clip_policy": {
        "basis": "y_true_quantiles",
        "q_low": 0.01,
        "q_high": 0.99,
        "note": (
            "Clipped metrics computed on subsets where true price is within "
            "[q_low, q_high] quantiles; predictions are never used for clipping."
        ),
    },
}

# Helper: safe string-or-None
def _str_or_none(x):
    return str(x) if x is not None else None

# Helper: build a split metrics object from globals
def _split_payload(full_key: str, clipped_key: str):
    full = globals().get(full_key, None)
    clipped = globals().get(clipped_key, None)
    return {
        "full": full,
        "clipped": clipped,
    }

# -------------------------
# Ridge metrics
# Requires you to set these in Ridge eval:
#   ridge_test_full, ridge_test_clipped
#   ridge_trainval_full, ridge_trainval_clipped
# -------------------------
if all(k in globals() for k in ["ridge_test_full", "ridge_test_clipped",
                                "ridge_trainval_full", "ridge_trainval_clipped"]):
    metrics["ridge"] = {
        "trainval": _split_payload("ridge_trainval_full", "ridge_trainval_clipped"),
        "test": _split_payload("ridge_test_full", "ridge_test_clipped"),
    }
else:
    print("ℹ️ Skipping Ridge metrics write (need ridge_*_full and ridge_*_clipped).")

# -------------------------
# CatBoost metrics
# Requires you to set these in CatBoost eval:
#   cb_test_full, cb_test_clipped
#   cb_trainval_full, cb_trainval_clipped
#   cb_train_full/cb_train_clipped, cb_val_full/cb_val_clipped
# -------------------------
if all(k in globals() for k in ["cb_test_full", "cb_test_clipped",
                                "cb_trainval_full", "cb_trainval_clipped"]):
    metrics["catboost"] = {
        "trainval": _split_payload("cb_trainval_full", "cb_trainval_clipped"),
        "test": _split_payload("cb_test_full", "cb_test_clipped"),
        "best_iteration": (
            int(final_cb.get_best_iteration())
            if ("final_cb" in globals() and final_cb is not None)
            else None
        ),
        "run_dir": _str_or_none(globals().get("RUN_DIR", None)),
    }

    # CV-best val metrics
    if all(name in globals() for name in ["mae_cv", "rmse_cv"]):
        metrics["catboost"]["val_cvbest"] = {
            "mae": float(mae_cv),
            "rmse": float(rmse_cv),
        }

    # Store explicit train/val split metrics if you created them
    if all(k in globals() for k in ["cb_train_full", "cb_train_clipped"]):
        metrics["catboost"]["train"] = _split_payload("cb_train_full", "cb_train_clipped")

    if all(k in globals() for k in ["cb_val_full", "cb_val_clipped"]):
        metrics["catboost"]["val"] = _split_payload("cb_val_full", "cb_val_clipped")

else:
    print("ℹ️ Skipping CatBoost metrics write (need cb_*_full and cb_*_clipped).")

# -------------------------
# Keras metrics
# Requires you to set these in Keras eval:
#   keras_test_full, keras_test_clipped
#   keras_trainval_full, keras_trainval_clipped
# -------------------------
if all(k in globals() for k in ["keras_test_full", "keras_test_clipped",
                                "keras_trainval_full", "keras_trainval_clipped"]):
    metrics["keras"] = {
        "trainval": _split_payload("keras_trainval_full", "keras_trainval_clipped"),
        "test": _split_payload("keras_test_full", "keras_test_clipped"),
        "run_dir": _str_or_none(globals().get("KERAS_RUN_DIR", None)),
    }
else:
    print("ℹ️ Skipping Keras metrics write (need keras_*_full and keras_*_clipped).")

# -------------------------
# Write files
# -------------------------
metrics_ts = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
METRICS_VERSIONED_PATH = METRICS_DIR / f"metrics_{metrics_ts}.json"

atomic_write_json(METRICS_PATH, metrics)
print("🧾 Wrote metrics:", METRICS_PATH)

atomic_write_json(METRICS_VERSIONED_PATH, metrics)
print("🧾 Wrote versioned metrics:", METRICS_VERSIONED_PATH)

# Run-specific metrics (CatBoost run dir)
if ("RUN_DIR" in globals()) and (globals().get("RUN_DIR", None) is not None):
    atomic_write_json(globals()["RUN_DIR"] / "metrics.json", metrics)
    print("🧾 Wrote run metrics:", globals()["RUN_DIR"] / "metrics.json")

# Run-specific metrics (Keras run dir)
if ("KERAS_RUN_DIR" in globals()) and (globals().get("KERAS_RUN_DIR", None) is not None):
    keras_only = {
        "_meta": metrics["_meta"],
        "keras": metrics.get("keras", None),
    }
    atomic_write_json(globals()["KERAS_RUN_DIR"] / "keras_metrics.json", keras_only)
    print("🧾 Wrote Keras-only metrics:", globals()["KERAS_RUN_DIR"] / "keras_metrics.json")

```

## Model Comparison Graphs

### Plotting Functions for Models
```{python}
# =========================
# Plot Function 1
# =========================

def clip_by_quantile(y_true, y_pred, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99):
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true)
    y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred)
    lo, hi = np.quantile(y_true, [q_low, q_high])
    mask = (y_true >= lo) & (y_true <= hi)
    return y_true[mask], y_pred[mask], mask, (lo, hi)

# =========================
# Plot Function 2
# =========================

def plot_regression_diagnostics(
    y_true, y_pred,
    X_ref=None, feature_for_residual="age",
    clip=False, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99,
    title_prefix=""
):
    """
    Plots:
      1) Predicted vs Actual
      2) Residuals vs Predicted
      3) Residuals vs a chosen feature (e.g. age or mileage)
      
    Clips by y_true quantiles for readability for some charts.
    """
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true)
    y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred)

    if clip:
        y_true_p, y_pred_p, mask, (lo, hi) = clip_by_quantile(y_true, y_pred, q_low, q_high)
        label_suffix = f" (clipped {int(q_low*100)}–{int(q_high*100)}% of y_true)"
    else:
        y_true_p, y_pred_p = y_true, y_pred
        mask = None
        label_suffix = " (unclipped)"

    # 1) Predicted vs Actual
    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 6))
    plt.scatter(y_true_p, y_pred_p, alpha=0.2)
    mn = min(y_true_p.min(), y_pred_p.min())
    mx = max(y_true_p.max(), y_pred_p.max())
    plt.plot([mn, mx], [mn, mx], "r--")
    plt.xlabel("Actual Price ($)")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Price ($)")
    plt.title(f"{title_prefix} Predicted vs Actual{label_suffix}")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    residuals = y_true_p - y_pred_p

    # 2) Residuals vs Predicted
    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
    plt.scatter(y_pred_p, residuals, alpha=0.2)
    plt.axhline(0, color="red", linestyle="--")
    plt.xlabel("Predicted Price ($)")
    plt.ylabel("Residual ($)  (actual - predicted)")
    plt.title(f"{title_prefix} Residuals vs Predicted{label_suffix}")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    # 3) Residuals vs Feature
    if X_ref is not None and feature_for_residual in X_ref.columns:
        if mask is not None:
            # mask aligns to y_true/y_pred order; apply same mask to X_ref
            x_feat = np.asarray(X_ref[feature_for_residual])[mask]
        else:
            x_feat = np.asarray(X_ref[feature_for_residual])

        plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
        plt.scatter(x_feat, residuals, alpha=0.2)
        plt.axhline(0, color="red", linestyle="--")
        plt.xlabel(feature_for_residual)
        plt.ylabel("Residual ($)")
        plt.title(f"{title_prefix} Residuals vs {feature_for_residual}{label_suffix}")
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()

# =========================
# Plot Function 3
# =========================

def plot_abs_error_vs_actual(
    y_true, y_pred,
    clip=False, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99,
    title_prefix="",
    log_x=True
):
    """
    Plot absolute error vs actual price.
    Clip based on y_true quantiles for readability (optional).
    Log-scale the x-axis (actual price).
    """
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true)
    y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred)

    if clip:
        y_true_p, y_pred_p, _, _ = clip_by_quantile(y_true, y_pred, q_low, q_high)
        label_suffix = f" (clipped {int(q_low*100)}–{int(q_high*100)}% of y_true)"
    else:
        y_true_p, y_pred_p = y_true, y_pred
        label_suffix = " (unclipped)"

    abs_err = np.abs(y_true_p - y_pred_p)

    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
    plt.scatter(y_true_p, abs_err, alpha=0.2)
    plt.ylabel("Absolute Error ($)")
    plt.title(f"{title_prefix} Absolute Error vs Actual{label_suffix}")
    if log_x:
        plt.xscale("log")
        plt.xlabel("Actual Price ($) [log scale]")
    else:
        plt.xlabel("Actual Price ($)")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

# =========================
# Plot Function 4
# =========================

def plot_pred_vs_pred(
    pred_a, pred_b,
    *,
    name_a="Model A",
    name_b="Model B",
    clip_by_y_true=None,   # pass y_true array to clip both preds by y_true quantiles
    clip=False,
    q_low=0.01,
    q_high=0.99
):
    """
    Scatter of predictions from two models against each other.
    Clipping is based on y_true quantiles (optional).
    """
    pred_a = np.asarray(pred_a)
    pred_b = np.asarray(pred_b)

    if clip:
        if clip_by_y_true is None:
            raise ValueError("clip=True requires clip_by_y_true (the y_true array).")
        y_true = np.asarray(clip_by_y_true)
        _, _, mask, _ = clip_by_quantile(y_true, pred_a, q_low=q_low, q_high=q_high)
        pred_a_p = pred_a[mask]
        pred_b_p = pred_b[mask]
        suffix = f" (clipped {int(q_low*100)}–{int(q_high*100)}% of y_true)"
    else:
        pred_a_p, pred_b_p = pred_a, pred_b
        suffix = " (unclipped)"

    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 6))
    plt.scatter(pred_a_p, pred_b_p, alpha=0.2)
    mn = min(pred_a_p.min(), pred_b_p.min())
    mx = max(pred_a_p.max(), pred_b_p.max())
    plt.plot([mn, mx], [mn, mx], "r--")
    plt.xlabel(f"{name_a} Predicted Price ($)")
    plt.ylabel(f"{name_b} Predicted Price ($)")
    plt.title(f"{name_a} vs {name_b} Predictions{suffix}")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

### Model Graph Section
```{python}
# =========================
# Model Comparison Plots -- using test set
# =========================

if ("X_test" not in globals()) or ("y_test" not in globals()):
    print("⚠️ X_test/y_test not found — cannot plot comparisons.")
else:
    y_true_test = np.expm1(y_test)

    # Choose feature for residual-vs-feature
    feature_name = "age" if "age" in X_test.columns else ("mileage" if "mileage" in X_test.columns else None)

    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    # Collect predictions from any models that exist
    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    preds = {}

    if ("best_ridge" in globals()) and (best_ridge is not None):
        preds["Ridge"] = np.expm1(best_ridge.predict(X_test))
    else:
        print("ℹ️ Ridge not available — skipping Ridge plots.")

    if ("final_cb" in globals()) and (final_cb is not None):
        preds["CatBoost"] = np.expm1(final_cb.predict(X_test))
    else:
        print("ℹ️ CatBoost not available — skipping CatBoost plots.")

    if ("model" in globals()) and (model is not None) and ("test_inputs" in globals()):
        y_pred_log_keras = model.predict(test_inputs, batch_size=4096, verbose=0).ravel()  # log1p preds
        preds["Keras"] = np.expm1(y_pred_log_keras)  # dollars
    else:
        print("ℹ️ Keras model/test_inputs not available — skipping Keras plots.")


    if len(preds) == 0:
        print("⚠️ No model predictions available — nothing to plot.")
    else:
        # ----------------------------------------------------------
        # 3 diagnostic plots + abs error plot for each model
        # (unclipped + clipped)
        # ----------------------------------------------------------
        for name, y_pred in preds.items():
            plot_regression_diagnostics(
                y_true_test, y_pred,
                X_ref=X_test,
                feature_for_residual=(feature_name or "age"),
                clip=False,
                title_prefix=f"{name} |"
            )
            plot_regression_diagnostics(
                y_true_test, y_pred,
                X_ref=X_test,
                feature_for_residual=(feature_name or "age"),
                clip=True, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99,
                title_prefix=f"{name} |"
            )

            plot_abs_error_vs_actual(
                y_true_test, y_pred,
                clip=False,
                title_prefix=f"{name} |",
                log_x=True
            )
            plot_abs_error_vs_actual(
                y_true_test, y_pred,
                clip=True, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99,
                title_prefix=f"{name} |",
                log_x=True
            )

        # ----------------------------------------------------------
        # 4th plot: pred vs pred for every pair of models
        # (unclipped + clipped)
        # ----------------------------------------------------------
        model_names = list(preds.keys())
        if len(model_names) >= 2:
            for i in range(len(model_names)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(model_names)):
                    a = model_names[i]
                    b = model_names[j]

                    plot_pred_vs_pred(
                        preds[a], preds[b],
                        name_a=a, name_b=b,
                        clip=False
                    )
                    plot_pred_vs_pred(
                        preds[a], preds[b],
                        name_a=a, name_b=b,
                        clip_by_y_true=y_true_test,
                        clip=True, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99
                    )
        else:
            print("ℹ️ Only one model available — skipping pred-vs-pred comparison plots.")
```
